violations.text: Tomatoes in the walk-in cooler were observed with an accumulation of mold build-up. Tomatoes were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2025
Tomatoes were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2025

The floor in the walk-in cooler area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2025

Salami, eggs, and fish was observed held between 46-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Salami, eggs, and fish were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
Bananas were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. Toilet room doors must be self closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025
Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were added at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

Chicken wings, empanadas, and chicharron was observed held at between 90-115 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All food was reheated to 165 DF or higher at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

Various food was observed held between 44-52 degrees Fahrenheit in the 2-door reach-in. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All food above 45 DF was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. All other food was moved to properly functioning refrigeration. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

Various food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025
Greens and onions were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

Knives were observed being stored between 3-bay sink and hand sink. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

Steam table #2 was observed not working and in poor repair. The 2 door reach-in was observed holding an ambient temperature of 51 DF and in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2025
Establishment is lacking proper drain plugs for the 3-bay sink. Establishment only has 2 drain plugs and must obtain another drain plug. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025

The plumbing for the 3-bay sink was observed in poor repair. When the sanitizer bay was being drained, water was being backed up into the wash bay. Plumbing must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/05/2025
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. Toilet rooms must be self closing. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/05/2024

Mangu and empanadas were observed held at 109-120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/05/2024

Working containers of flour, sugar and spices are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 07/05/2024
Storage rack located in the ware washing area was not in good repair, observed splintering. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2024
